blackroot_thorne:idle:default.greeting: He is sitting on a stump that wasn't here when you arrived. The bark is gray and the leaves around him are wrong-coloured. He doesn't look up when you stop.

nodes.npcText: Speak softly. The forest where I came from died loud, and I have not forgiven loud noises since.

playerOptions.text: What do you think of the Sovereign?

nodes.npcText: The Verdant Veil. He refused it, and most of it stopped existing. What remains is mine to keep alive. I do not have the years to waste on people who do not understand that.
Because she IS the wild. She could have refused the refusal. She did not. She has her reasons. They are not mine.
Supplies. Information. People who hate the Sovereign and might be useful. I do not enjoy this place. I tolerate it.
I think one day I will kill him. I think I will fail. I think I will try anyway.
Few are. Thank you for the smaller of them.

voss_seraphine:idle:default.greeting: She is in the salon, half-turned, a glass of something deep red in one hand. She looks at you the way a cat looks at a window.

nodes.npcText: Charming of you to interrupt. Sit, please. I'd ask your business but I assume it changes once you see who's here.

playerOptions.text: Are you related to Elias Voss?

nodes.npcText: The Crimson Court holds the most useful kind of power: the kind that doesn't need to introduce itself. We trade in debts, marriages, and the occasional weather pattern. You may pay me a compliment, if it helps.
I have, on three separate occasions, decided whether to seduce or destroy him. The decision keeps changing. He's bad for me either way. I find that delicious.
We share a name. We do not, in public, share much else. Don't ask Elias. He won't tell you and he'll like you less for asking.
Mind the doors. Some of them owe me something.
